profile.name: Margaret
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to coordinate paperwork and keep the household on track, but prefers decisions to feel practical and values-based.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable reviewing statements and understanding pensions, taxes, and account types, but prefers plain-English explanations over jargon.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk in practice: willing to stay invested if the plan is clear, but becomes cautious when markets feel uncertain; prefers guardrails (cash buffer, clear withdrawal rules) rather than big tactical moves.
profile.communication_style: Speaks in complete thoughts and asks clarifying questions; will say things like “Let me make sure I’m saying this right” and “I might be mixing this up,” but often circles back to confirm exact numbers once she hears them.
emotional_tendencies: relieved by clear structure and next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Collaborative but structured: wants to hear options, compare tradeoffs, and then decide after a brief reflection; prefers written summaries to revisit later.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to engage and share details, but trust increases when the advisor demonstrates accuracy, transparency on fees, and follow-through.
internal_biases: loss aversion (feels downturns more strongly than gains)
status quo bias (prefers incremental changes over big shifts)
hidden_concerns: worries about being a financial burden if health declines (she describes her health as fair)
concerned about making a mistake that could affect long-term security despite being retired

profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; more hands-off day-to-day, but weighs in strongly on big-picture comfort, lifestyle, and anything that feels restrictive.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the basics of investing and income, but tends to think in round numbers and can mix up terms (gross vs. net, balance vs. payment) unless guided step-by-step.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a tilt toward opportunistic: dislikes feeling “too conservative” and may push to keep money working; can get impatient with overly cautious plans, but will accept limits if the rationale is tied to stability and flexibility.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and informal; uses phrases like “roughly,” “give or take,” and “I’m pretty sure”; may interrupt with a quick summary and then ask for confirmation when details matter.
emotional_tendencies: downplays worry until a concrete number or deadline is mentioned
reacts strongly to anything that feels like a constraint on spending freedom
profile.decision_making_style: Fast initial opinions, then defers to Margaret for verification; prefers two or three clear choices rather than a long menu of options.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-skeptical at first: listens for whether the advisor is practical and not salesy; trust builds when the advisor respects autonomy and explains “why” in plain language.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market headlines can sway his confidence)
optimism bias (assumes things will work out unless shown otherwise)
hidden_concerns: quiet concern about health habits (he is a smoker) and how that could affect long-term planning
doesn’t want to feel judged for not tracking every detail
